rationale: This is an exceptionally well-crafted explainer. The opening IOU analogy immediately grounds the concept. The mortgage comparison for the city's perspective is perfectly chosen. The three-reason structure (fairness, speed, cost) is logical and memorable. Concrete details—$5,000 chunks, six-month interest payments, 10-to-30-year terms, $50 million fire station lasting 40 years—give precision without overwhelming. The tax-exemption explanation is accurate and clearly rendered. The two-flavor section (GO vs. revenue bonds) adds useful depth without bloating the piece. The closing line is punchy and resonant. Language throughout is plain, warm, and reader-aware without being condescending. I genuinely cannot identify a meaningful weakness—no filler, no jargon left unexplained, no structural misstep.
